From Wikipedia, the free encyclopedia

SFScope

|  |  |
| --- | --- |
| Available in | English |
| Owner | [Ian Randal Strock](/wiki/Ian_Randal_Strock "Ian Randal Strock") |
| URL | <http://sfscope.com/> |
| Launched | 2007 |
| Current status | Inactive |

**SFScope** is an [online trade journal](/wiki/Trade_magazine "Trade magazine")[[1]](#cite_note-strockbook-1) devoted to entertainment news concerning [speculative fiction](/wiki/Speculative_fiction "Speculative fiction"), [science fiction](/wiki/Science_fiction "Science fiction"), fantasy, and horror.[[2]](#cite_note-aboutsfscope-2) It was founded by [Ian Randal Strock](/wiki/Ian_Randal_Strock "Ian Randal Strock") in early 2007.

Strock began his career as the editorial assistant of the magazines *[Analog Science Fiction and Fact](/wiki/Analog_Science_Fiction_and_Fact "Analog Science Fiction and Fact")* and *[Asimov's Science Fiction](/wiki/Asimov%27s_Science_Fiction "Asimov's Science Fiction")*.[[3]](#cite_note-Filmcritic-3) He founded SFScope in early 2007 to "deliver the news of the speculative fiction fields in a timely, accessible fashion."[[3]](#cite_note-Filmcritic-3) While he is the site's publisher and primary editor,[[1]](#cite_note-strockbook-1) writers Kit Hawkins, [Michael A. Burstein](/wiki/Michael_A._Burstein "Michael A. Burstein"), and Sarah Stegall also contribute columns.[[2]](#cite_note-aboutsfscope-2)

Strock has stated, "We shy away from the constant rumor updates about coming movies. There are so many other sites already covering that aspect of the field."[[3]](#cite_note-Filmcritic-3)

## Hiatus

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=SFScope&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Hiatus")]

The last legitimate post to this site is dated 7 July 2014, titled ‘Sorry for the Silence’[[1]](https://www.sfscope.com/2014/07/sorry-for-the-silence/) by Ian Randal Strock.

As of 24 August 2019, the main page of the site has been hijacked by spammers and publishes a steady stream of dating sites and so forth.
